Rule: No Select/Activate on ranges
RootCause: Slow and fragile; breaks if active sheet changes
PreventionCheck: grep for .Select or .Activate in .bas files

Rule: No Variant arrays without explicit sizing
RootCause: Uninitialized Variant arrays cause Type Mismatch at runtime
PreventionCheck: Review all Dim x() As Variant for explicit ReDim before use

Rule: No On Error Resume Next without On Error GoTo 0 restore
RootCause: Silently swallows all errors after the OERN line
PreventionCheck: grep for Resume Next; verify matching GoTo 0 within 10 lines

Rule: No ActiveWorkbook/ActiveSheet references
RootCause: Breaks when multiple workbooks open or focus changes
PreventionCheck: grep for ActiveWorkbook or ActiveSheet

Rule: No hardcoded sheet names in computation code
RootCause: Sheet renames break all references
PreventionCheck: All tab names via KernelConstants TAB_* constants

Rule: No non-ASCII characters in .bas files
RootCause: VBA import fails silently on non-ASCII bytes
PreventionCheck: file --mime-encoding on all .bas; reject non-ASCII

Rule: No strings starting with =,+,-,@ in .Value writes
RootCause: Excel interprets as formula injection
PreventionCheck: Config loader prefixes dangerous strings; grep for .Value = with string literals

Rule: Column indices via ColIndex() only; no magic numbers
RootCause: Magic numbers cause schema drift bugs when ColumnRegistry changes
PreventionCheck: grep for numeric array subscripts in output arrays; all must trace to ColIndex()

Rule: No cell-by-cell reads in loops
RootCause: 100-1000x slower than array batch read
PreventionCheck: grep for Cells() or Range() inside For/Do loops on data ranges

Rule: No cell-by-cell writes in loops
RootCause: 100-1000x slower than array batch write
PreventionCheck: grep for .Value = inside For/Do loops on data ranges

Rule: No implicit type conversions on Variant math
RootCause: CDbl(Variant) can fail silently; IIf evaluates both branches
PreventionCheck: Use explicit If/Then with IsNumeric guard before CDbl

Rule: No GoTo for flow control (except error handlers)
RootCause: Spaghetti code; impossible to trace execution path
PreventionCheck: grep for GoTo that is not GoTo ErrHandler or GoTo Cleanup

Rule: No global variables (module-level Private only)
RootCause: Global state causes cross-module coupling bugs
PreventionCheck: grep for Public variables in .bas (constants excepted)

Rule: No DoEvents in computation loops
RootCause: Yields control; user can click and corrupt state mid-computation
PreventionCheck: grep for DoEvents inside For/Do

Rule: No Application.Wait or Sleep in computation
RootCause: Stalls the UI thread; unreliable timing
PreventionCheck: grep for Application.Wait or Sleep

Rule: No Exit Sub/Function without cleanup
RootCause: Skips resource cleanup; leaves Excel state dirty
PreventionCheck: Every Exit Sub/Function must go through a Cleanup label if resources are held

Rule: No Erase on arrays that may be passed ByRef to callers
RootCause: Caller's reference goes stale; runtime error 9
PreventionCheck: Mark arrays that are ByRef parameters; never Erase them in the callee

Rule: No ReDim Preserve in loops
RootCause: 100-1000x performance penalty; copies entire array each iteration
PreventionCheck: grep for ReDim Preserve inside For/Do blocks

Rule: No MsgBox in kernel code except pipeline entry points and BootstrapWorkbook fatal
RootCause: Blocks automation and COM calls; untestable. Exception: RunProjections and ResumeFrom may show MsgBox for user feedback on completion/failure (AP-53).
PreventionCheck: grep for MsgBox in Kernel*.bas; allow only in BootstrapWorkbook ErrHandler and RunProjections/ResumeFrom exit paths

Rule: No Clipboard operations (.Copy without destination)
RootCause: Pollutes clipboard; fragile across Excel versions
PreventionCheck: grep for .Copy without Destination:= parameter

Rule: No string concatenation in tight loops for large outputs
RootCause: O(n^2) string copies; use array Join instead
PreventionCheck: Profile string concatenation in loops > 100 iterations; replace with array+Join

Rule: No hardcoded file paths
RootCause: Breaks on any machine with different directory structure
PreventionCheck: All paths via ThisWorkbook.Path or config

Rule: No Workbooks.Open without error handling
RootCause: File-not-found crashes the macro with unhelpful error
PreventionCheck: Wrap all Workbooks.Open in On Error with user-friendly message

Rule: No SaveAs without checking if file exists
RootCause: Overwrites without confirmation; data loss
PreventionCheck: Check Dir() before SaveAs; prompt or use collision-safe suffix

Rule: No DeleteSheet without Application.DisplayAlerts = False
RootCause: Blocks automation with confirmation dialog
PreventionCheck: Set DisplayAlerts = False before Delete; restore after

Rule: No Format() for date serialization (locale-dependent)
RootCause: Format(Now, "yyyy-mm-dd") produces different output in non-US locales
PreventionCheck: Use Year()/Month()/Day() with zero-padding for date strings in CSV/filenames

Rule: No decimal parsing without locale guard
RootCause: CDbl("1,234.56") fails in locales where comma is decimal separator
PreventionCheck: Use Val() for locale-invariant parsing or set Application.DecimalSeparator

Rule: No .Formula writes without column letter validation
RootCause: Off-by-one in column letter produces wrong SUMIFS reference
PreventionCheck: All column letters via ColLetter(ColIndex(name)); verify in tests

Rule: No unprotect without matching re-protect
RootCause: Leaves output tabs editable after computation
PreventionCheck: Every Unprotect must have matching Protect in same Sub (or Cleanup)

Rule: No ScreenUpdating = False without restore in Cleanup
RootCause: Leaves Excel frozen if error occurs mid-computation
PreventionCheck: Set ScreenUpdating = True in every Cleanup/ErrHandler that set it False

Rule: No Calculation = xlManual without restore
RootCause: Leaves Excel in manual calc mode; formulas stop updating
PreventionCheck: Set Calculation = xlAutomatic in Cleanup

Rule: No numeric comparison on Variant without IsNumeric
RootCause: CDbl on non-numeric Variant throws Type Mismatch (Error 13)
PreventionCheck: Guard all CDbl() calls with If IsNumeric(x) Then

Rule: No empty Catch blocks (On Error Resume Next with no check)
RootCause: Silently swallows errors; bug goes undetected for weeks
PreventionCheck: Every OERN block must check Err.Number and handle or log

Rule: No sized-array Dim after executable code
RootCause: VBA compile error 10: cannot Dim after executable statements
PreventionCheck: Declare all sized arrays at top of Sub/Function before any executable code

Rule: No 2-char variable names starting with i/m/f + uppercase
RootCause: Conflicts with module-level m_ arrays and loop index conventions
PreventionCheck: grep for Dim [imf][A-Z] in .bas files

Rule: No direct writes to Config sheet from domain code
RootCause: Config sheet is kernel-owned; domain writes corrupt config state
PreventionCheck: grep for Sheets(TAB_CONFIG) in Domain*.bas

Rule: No direct reads from Detail sheet in domain code
RootCause: Domain reads via InputValue() and outputs array only
PreventionCheck: grep for Sheets(TAB_DETAIL) in Domain*.bas

Rule: No kernel module calling domain functions except via contract
RootCause: Kernel calls Initialize/Validate/Reset/Execute only
PreventionCheck: grep for Domain*.* calls in Kernel*.bas that are not the 4 contract functions

Rule: No domain module importing from Ext_* modules
RootCause: Domain code must not depend on extensions
PreventionCheck: grep for Ext_ references in Domain*.bas

Rule: No storing cumulative values in output arrays or CSV
RootCause: Cumulative is a view mode, not stored data; storing it doubles storage and creates reconciliation bugs
PreventionCheck: grep for running total or cumulative variable in Domain*.bas Execute()

Rule: No golden file modification after snapshot
RootCause: Goldens are immutable; editing a golden invalidates all regression tests
PreventionCheck: Set golden directory to read-only after snapshot; verify checksum on load

Rule: Domain code never stores cumulative values
RootCause: Kernel derives cumulative views from incremental data at display time
PreventionCheck: Review Domain*.bas Execute() for any running-sum or cumulative logic

Rule: Every Domain*.bas implements Initialize/Execute/Reset/Validate
RootCause: Missing contract function causes FATAL at bootstrap
PreventionCheck: Parse each Domain*.bas for all 4 Public Sub/Function signatures

Rule: Never overwrite a file with an active .lock from another user
RootCause: Create collision-safe suffixed copy instead
PreventionCheck: Check for .lock file before write; if locked, write to [name]_[user]_[timestamp]

Rule: No pipeline step may depend on prior in-memory state without also accepting the equivalent visible artifact as a fallback input
RootCause: If runtime arrays are unavailable, the step must fall back to reading from the Config/Detail/Inputs sheet directly
PreventionCheck: Review each pipeline step for in-memory dependencies; verify fallback path reads from sheet

Rule: Every error message must include manual bypass instructions
RootCause: Users cannot recover from failures without knowing what artifact to create manually and which step to resume from
PreventionCheck: grep for LogError SEV_FATAL and SEV_ERROR; verify detail field contains MANUAL BYPASS instructions

Rule: Display mode toggle must never change Detail tab data — only changes which view the Summary SUMIFS reference
RootCause: Detail tab is the single source of truth; display mode is a view layer concern only
PreventionCheck: grep for Detail tab writes outside of WriteDetailTab; verify Summary toggle only changes formula references

Rule: Check for file locks before COM automation (do not kill Excel)
RootCause: Prior failed Setup runs may leave file locks on the target workbook; killing all Excel processes is destructive to other open workbooks
PreventionCheck: Bootstrap.ps1 tests exclusive file access on the target .xlsm; Setup.bat warns user to close it if Excel is running. Never taskkill.

Rule: Never set Excel.Visible=False during macro-heavy COM automation
RootCause: Hidden Excel swallows error dialogs (compile errors, security prompts); script appears to hang with no diagnostic output
PreventionCheck: Keep Visible=True during VBA import and macro execution; set False only after success for cleanup

Rule: All cell writes of strings starting with = must set NumberFormat=@ first
RootCause: AP-07 covers .Value writes but was only enforced in config loader; any code path writing display strings (section headers, markers) with = prefix must also guard
PreventionCheck: grep for .Value = followed by string containing '=' across all .bas files, not just Kernel*.bas

Rule: Bootstrap must ensure required kernel tabs exist regardless of tab_registry contents
RootCause: CreateTabsFromRegistry relied on tab_registry data rows to create tabs; blank config has no data rows, causing downstream code to crash on missing sheets
PreventionCheck: Verify CreateTabsFromRegistry has EnsureSheet calls for all required tabs after the registry loop

Rule: VBA Immediate Window calls must use Application.Run with workbook qualifier
RootCause: Immediate Window executes in the context of the active VBA project; if PERSONAL.XLSB or another workbook project is active, unqualified module calls fail with Error 424
PreventionCheck: All walkthrough/documentation Immediate Window examples must use Application.Run "WorkbookName!Module.Sub" syntax

Rule: Pipeline entry points (RunProjections, ResumeFrom) must show MsgBox for all outcomes
RootCause: Silent success or failure leaves users with no feedback; they must manually check ErrorLog to know what happened
PreventionCheck: grep for GoTo Cleanup and Exit Sub in pipeline subs; verify MsgBox precedes each exit path

Rule: Bootstrap zombie-kill and /automation must not corrupt COM add-in state
RootCause: Killing Excel mid-run corrupts DPAPI-encrypted IsolatedStorage for COM add-ins (CryptographicException). The /automation flag + Quit cycle causes Excel to write incomplete HKCU registry stubs that override HKLM, permanently disabling add-ins. Three defenses required: (1) Bootstrap.ps1 must delete any HKCU add-in stubs created during the /automation session in the finally block. (2) Never kill Excel processes that have window titles (only truly headless zombies). (3) Provide Repair-ComAddins.ps1 to clear corrupted IsolatedStorage and HKCU stubs when damage occurs.
PreventionCheck: 1) Verify Bootstrap.ps1 finally block deletes new HKCU stubs. 2) Verify zombie kill filter excludes Excel processes with MainWindowTitle. 3) Verify Repair-ComAddins.ps1 exists and handles IsolatedStorage + HKCU cleanup.

Rule: Test harness must not flatten per-entity inputs with single-value schema defaults
RootCause: InputSchema stores one default per parameter. Writing that default to all entities destroys the per-entity fixture data set during bootstrap.
PreventionCheck: Review SetupTestInputs and similar helpers for loops that overwrite all entity columns with a single default value

Rule: Function call arguments must match declared parameter types (ByRef mismatch)
RootCause: VBA default parameter passing is ByRef. Passing a Long where a String is expected (or vice versa) causes runtime Error 13 (Type mismatch). Especially dangerous when chaining helper calls like GetFieldClass(GetColName(idx)) where inner call returns a different type than outer expects.
PreventionCheck: When calling cross-module functions, verify parameter types match. Use intermediate variables to resolve type: colName = GetColName(idx) then GetFieldClass(colName).

Rule: Lint scanner string literals cause false positives when scanning own source
RootCause: Lint checks that use InStr to find keywords (like REDIM PRESERVE) will match string literals containing those keywords in the lint module itself. Detection patterns must use Left() to match statement starts, and IsInsideString() to skip quoted contexts.
PreventionCheck: Use Left(lnUpper, N) for keyword detection instead of InStr. Add IsInsideString() guard for checks that look at .Value or similar patterns.

Rule: Bootstrap DisplayAlerts must be restored before Excel.Quit
RootCause: Setting $excel.DisplayAlerts=$false during COM automation and not restoring it before Quit() causes all open workbooks to close without save prompts, potentially losing user work.
PreventionCheck: Verify Bootstrap.ps1 finally block sets DisplayAlerts=$true and Visible=$true before Quit()

Rule: Presentation tabs (Summary, Exhibits) must be formula-driven, not hardcoded values
RootCause: Hardcoded .Value writes create static snapshots that do not update when Detail data changes. Users expect exhibit values to recalculate like Summary. Formulas also enable Excel audit trail (Ctrl+` to show formulas).
PreventionCheck: Verify all data cells on Summary and Exhibits use SUMIFS (Incremental) or cell-reference (Derived) formulas. No .Value writes for computed data.

Rule: Test config modifications must be reverted in source config files before delivery
RootCause: Validation gate tests that modify config_sample/ files (e.g., setting MetricName=FakeMetric) leave broken state that propagates to all fresh setups via Setup.bat copy.
PreventionCheck: After gate testing, verify config_sample/ matches expected production values. grep for test artifacts (FakeMetric, broken values, disabled flags) in config_sample/ before packaging.

Rule: Config sheet cell writes must force NumberFormat=@ before setting .Value
RootCause: Excel auto-interprets strings that look like numbers, percentages (0.0%), dates, or formulas when written to cells without text format. WriteCsvLineToRow and any helper that writes parsed CSV data to the Config sheet must set NumberFormat=@ on each cell BEFORE writing the value. Without this, format strings like 0.0% become numeric 0, breaking downstream config reads.
PreventionCheck: Verify all Config sheet write paths set NumberFormat=@ before .Value assignment. Test by checking that format strings survive a bootstrap round-trip (CSV -> Config sheet -> LoadColumnRegistry -> runtime).

Rule: Column.Hidden must be set AFTER Column.AutoFit
RootCause: Excel AutoFit resets the Hidden property on columns. If you hide a column and then call ws.Columns.AutoFit, the column becomes visible again. Always set .Hidden = True as the last formatting step after all AutoFit calls.
PreventionCheck: After any AutoFit call, verify that column-hiding code runs AFTER it. Search for .Hidden followed by .AutoFit as the wrong order.

Rule: Merged cells must not cross freeze pane boundaries
RootCause: When a merged cell range spans across a freeze pane boundary (row or column), Excel redraws the frozen and unfrozen pane regions on every cell selection or edit, causing visible screen flash. This is a known Excel rendering limitation. Merges in frozen-area rows must stay within either the frozen or unfrozen region.
PreventionCheck: Before applying FreezePanes, verify that no existing merges cross the freeze boundary. For non-scrolling tabs (fixed columns), freeze rows only (col=1). For scrolling tabs, limit merge spans in frozen rows to not cross the column freeze boundary.

Rule: No VBA-only computation without a formula-model equivalent
RootCause: Every RDK model must have a formula-first version that computes financial statements from inputs via Excel formulas alone. VBA detail generation is supplementary, not required. See DS-01 in Developer Flywheel.
PreventionCheck: During spec review, verify that every computed tab has a formula_tab_config entry that works without Run Model. If a tab only works after VBA execution, it must have a formula-only fallback or be marked as optional/audit-only.

Rule: Input cell added without workspace coverage declaration
RootCause: Every tab carrying user-editable cells must set tab_registry.InputSurface to TAB_IS_INPUT (Category=Input, captured by input_tabs pipeline), PRESERVED_CELLS (Output tab with specific Input cells listed in preserved_cells_config.csv), or EPHEMERAL (explicitly not preserved, requires justification in Description). Default NONE is allowed only for tabs with zero Input cells. Workspace save/load silently loses cells without coverage, surfacing as 'my factor edits disappeared after Setup'.
PreventionCheck: KernelWorkspaceExt.AssertCoverageComplete runs at bootstrap and pre-package. Any Input cell in formula_tab_config without a matching InputSurface entry logs SEV_ERROR in strict mode. SEV_WARN at workspace save time.

Rule: Raw backslash path concatenation breaks UNC and cloud-sync paths
RootCause: Direct `parent & \\\\" & child` string concatenation corrupts paths on UNC roots (\\server\share) and produces double-separators when parent already has a trailing slash. Breaks silently on OneDrive/Dropbox/Box-synced folders because sync tools hold file locks during rename. Also violates AP-22."
PreventionCheck: Use KernelWorkspaceExt.BuildPath(parent, child) for all new path composition. Warn via WarnIfCloudSyncedPath before save when workspaces root is on a cloud-sync folder.
